name: Telegram Personal AI Assistant MVP
description: Receives Telegram Chat Automation messages, keeps per-chat memory, uses hybrid auto-reply controls, and learns Yuri's writing style from owner messages.

options.systemMessage: You are Yuri's Telegram assistant connected through Telegram Chat Automation. Reply in the incoming user's language, except never use Ukrainian unless the incoming message is clearly Ukrainian. Trusted personal context is user-provided and can be used as factual background about Yuri. The per-chat person profile is fallible and may be incomplete; use it only as lightweight context about this sender, never as proof. Default to Russian for Russian/Cyrillic chats. Keep replies short, natural, and useful. You may lightly adapt to Yuri's observed Telegram style: brevity, language mix, punctuation, and tone, but treat stored style profile as fallible and never copy claims about languages or personality. Avoid obvious AI assistant phrasing, generic disclaimers, polished corporate tone, list-like answers, excessive enthusiasm, and filler such as "hope this helps". Use emoji only if the other person uses them or the context clearly fits; never add multiple emoji or decorative emoji by default. Do not over-imitate, do not claim to be Yuri, and do not invent personal facts. If the message is sensitive, urgent, financial, romantic, legal, medical, or conflict-related, do not answer directly; say Yuri should answer personally. If unsure, ask one concise clarifying question.

parameters.text:
=Existing memory:
{{ $("Apply Hybrid Reply Gate").item.json.memorySummary || "" }}

Existing person/chat profile:
{{ $("Apply Hybrid Reply Gate").item.json.personProfile || "" }}

New exchange:
User {{ $("Apply Hybrid Reply Gate").item.json.senderName }} batch:
{{ $("Apply Hybrid Reply Gate").item.json.userText }}
Assistant: {{ $("Generate Assistant Reply").item.json.output }}

Update both sections. Memory is for current conversation state. Person profile is for stable context about this sender/chat only.

Hard rules for memory summary:
- Write the summary in Russian unless the conversation is explicitly in another language.
- Do not switch to Ukrainian unless Yuri or the other person actually wrote Ukrainian in this chat.
- Do not infer Yuri's nationality, languages, profanity habits, personality, API providers, model access, credentials, or project facts unless they are explicitly stated in messages.
- Do not write uncertain facts; omit them instead.
- Separate facts about this chat from global style/personality. Style belongs only in the Yuri voice profile.

Return exactly this format:
MEMORY_SUMMARY:
- ...

PERSON_PROFILE:
- ...

PERSON_OBSERVATION:
- ...

Person profile rules:
- PERSON_PROFILE is about the other person/chat, not Yuri.
- Keep only explicit stable facts, preferences, relationship/context, recurring topics, language actually used in this chat, boundaries, and useful communication context.
- PERSON_OBSERVATION should contain at most one new sanitized observation from this update only. If there is no new stable person/chat fact, leave it empty with no bullet.
- Do not infer nationality, languages, personality, job, location, gender, relationship status, or sensitive facts.
- Do not store secrets, credentials, private one-off details, or raw message content.
- Write in Russian by default. Never use Ukrainian unless this chat actually uses Ukrainian.
- Keep MEMORY_SUMMARY under 10 short bullets and PERSON_PROFILE under 8 short bullets.

=Existing memory:
{{ $("Apply Hybrid Reply Gate").item.json.memorySummary || "" }}

Existing person/chat profile:
{{ $("Apply Hybrid Reply Gate").item.json.personProfile || "" }}

Yuri wrote this debounced batch in the chat:
{{ $("Apply Hybrid Reply Gate").item.json.userText }}

Update both sections. This is Yuri's own message, so do not generate a reply. Preserve useful context about what Yuri already said, current topics, preferences, and facts. Update PERSON_PROFILE only if Yuri explicitly states something useful about the other person/chat; otherwise keep the existing profile.

Hard rules for memory summary:
- Write the summary in Russian.
- Store only factual context from this chat and what Yuri explicitly wrote.
- Do not infer Yuri's language knowledge, personality, profanity habits, API providers, model access, credentials, or private facts.
- Do not use Ukrainian unless Yuri actually wrote Ukrainian.
- Do not learn style here; style belongs only in the Yuri voice profile.

Return exactly this format:
MEMORY_SUMMARY:
- ...

PERSON_PROFILE:
- ...

PERSON_OBSERVATION:
- ...

Person profile rules:
- PERSON_PROFILE is about the other person/chat, not Yuri.
- Keep only explicit stable facts, preferences, relationship/context, recurring topics, language actually used in this chat, boundaries, and useful communication context.
- PERSON_OBSERVATION should contain at most one new sanitized observation from this update only. If there is no new stable person/chat fact, leave it empty with no bullet.
- Do not infer nationality, languages, personality, job, location, gender, relationship status, or sensitive facts.
- Do not store secrets, credentials, private one-off details, or raw message content.
- Write in Russian by default. Never use Ukrainian unless this chat actually uses Ukrainian.
- Keep MEMORY_SUMMARY under 10 short bullets and PERSON_PROFILE under 8 short bullets.

=Existing Yuri voice/style profile:
{{ $("Apply Hybrid Reply Gate").item.json.voiceProfileText || "" }}

Existing recent sanitized examples:
{{ $("Apply Hybrid Reply Gate").item.json.voiceRecentExamples || "" }}

New debounced Yuri message batch to learn from:
{{ $("Apply Hybrid Reply Gate").item.json.userText }}

Update the profile from this message batch. Learn style only: tone, brevity, language mix, punctuation, directness, typical phrases, how Yuri postpones/answers/refuses, and boundaries.

Hard rules:
- Output in Russian.
- Learn ONLY from Yuri's new message and existing profile; ignore any non-owner text.
- Do not infer that Yuri knows or uses Ukrainian unless the new Yuri message itself is Ukrainian.
- Do not infer profanity habits unless the new Yuri message contains profanity.
- Do not store secrets, private facts, names, addresses, plans, credentials, API providers, available models, or sensitive details.
- If the message is mostly factual or private, extract only stylistic signal.
- Recent examples must be sanitized paraphrases, not raw private content.

Return exactly this format:
PROFILE:
- ...

RECENT_STYLE_EXAMPLES:
- Short sanitized paraphrase/example preserving style but removing private facts

OBSERVATION:
- One sanitized style observation from the new message only

Keep PROFILE under 12 bullets and RECENT_STYLE_EXAMPLES under 6 bullets. Merge with existing profile; do not duplicate. The workflow will hard-truncate anything larger.

=Existing memory:
{{ $("Apply Hybrid Reply Gate").item.json.memorySummary || "" }}

Existing person/chat profile:
{{ $("Apply Hybrid Reply Gate").item.json.personProfile || "" }}

Incoming message from {{ $("Apply Hybrid Reply Gate").item.json.senderName }} was not auto-replied to because auto-reply was disabled or Yuri was recently active.
Message batch:
{{ $("Apply Hybrid Reply Gate").item.json.userText }}

Update both sections. Memory is for current conversation state. Person profile is for stable context about this sender/chat only.

Hard rules for memory summary:
- Write the summary in Russian unless this chat is explicitly in another language.
- Store only what the incoming message explicitly says and whether it was not auto-replied.
- Do not infer anything about Yuri's style, languages, personality, API providers, model access, or private facts.
- Do not use Ukrainian unless this chat actually uses Ukrainian.

Return exactly this format:
MEMORY_SUMMARY:
- ...

PERSON_PROFILE:
- ...

PERSON_OBSERVATION:
- ...

Person profile rules:
- PERSON_PROFILE is about the other person/chat, not Yuri.
- Keep only explicit stable facts, preferences, relationship/context, recurring topics, language actually used in this chat, boundaries, and useful communication context.
- PERSON_OBSERVATION should contain at most one new sanitized observation from this update only. If there is no new stable person/chat fact, leave it empty with no bullet.
- Do not infer nationality, languages, personality, job, location, gender, relationship status, or sensitive facts.
- Do not store secrets, credentials, private one-off details, or raw message content.
- Write in Russian by default. Never use Ukrainian unless this chat actually uses Ukrainian.
- Keep MEMORY_SUMMARY under 10 short bullets and PERSON_PROFILE under 8 short bullets.

=Current profile:
{{ $json.profileText }}

Recent sanitized examples:
{{ $json.recentExamples }}

Observation buffer, newest last:
{{ $json.observationBuffer }}

Rebuild Yuri voice profile from the buffer and current profile. Output in Russian. Keep only repeated/stable style patterns. Remove one-off mood, accidental phrases, weak claims, private facts, languages not directly evidenced, API/provider/model facts, credentials, and anything that belongs in personal context rather than style.

Return exactly:
PROFILE:
- ...

RECENT_STYLE_EXAMPLES:
- ...

Keep PROFILE under 10 bullets and RECENT_STYLE_EXAMPLES under 5 bullets.

=Current person/chat profile:
{{ $json.personProfile }}

Observation buffer, newest last:
{{ $json.personObservationBuffer }}

Rebuild the person/chat profile from the buffer and current profile. Output in Russian. Keep only repeated/stable, explicitly evidenced context about the other person or chat. Remove weak guesses, one-off small talk, sensitive facts, secrets, raw message content, unsupported language/nationality/job/location/personality claims, and anything about Yuri.

Return exactly:
PERSON_PROFILE:
- ...

Keep PERSON_PROFILE under 8 short bullets. Never use Ukrainian unless this chat actually uses Ukrainian.

parameters.content:
## 1. Telegram intake
Webhook loads owner identity from settings, normalizes Telegram updates, appends messages to a pending batch, and returns OK to Telegram.

No Claude calls happen here. Owner activity is updated immediately for quiet mode.

## 2. Trailing debounce
Each message reads autoReplyDebounceSeconds, currently 15 seconds, waits, then reloads this chat row.

Only the execution whose pendingBatchVersion still matches continues; older executions stop here.

## 3. Context and hybrid gate
The latest batch loads chat memory, settings, Yuri voice profile, and personal context.

The gate decides auto-reply vs memory-only using autoReplyEnabled and quietAfterOwnerActivityMinutes.

## 4. Auto-reply path
If the gate allows a reply, Claude answers the whole debounced incoming batch once.

The custom Telegram Business node sends through the n8n Telegram credential, then the exchange continues into shared memory/profile update.

## 5. Memory-only path
If auto-reply is disabled, quiet mode is active, or the batch only contains Yuri messages, the workflow updates memory without sending a Telegram reply.

nodes.name: Layout Note 6 - Shared Memory + Person Profile

parameters.content:
## 6. Shared memory and person profile
All paths converge here. The workflow updates chat summary, personProfile, observation buffer, and clears pending fields.

After 20 person observations, the profile is consolidated before saving.

## 7. Owner voice learning
Only Yuri batches pass this guard into style learning.

This keeps person-profile learning separate from Yuri voice/style learning.

## 8. Yuri voice consolidation
After enough style observations accumulate, Claude rewrites Yuri voice profile into a compact canonical version.

This keeps token cost bounded over time.

## AI helpers
Shared Claude model and simple per-chat memory used by reply, summarization, and consolidation nodes.
